pages.title: About | Artisanal Arsonist Pottery
paragraphs: You don’t have any items in your cart.

I draw inspiration from connecting with the incredible and diverse natural beauty of Vancouver Island and the Salish Sea shores, which I am fortunate to call home.The name Artisanal Arsonist is derived from a back-country experience with friends, as a result of my campfire building skills. I take great joy in “firing” my art and hope that you love your pottery for many years to come.I am delighted to share the results of my deeply rooted creativity with you.

pages.title: Food Safe Glaze Info & Care Instructions | Artisanal Arsonist Pottery
h2: Food Safe Glaze Info & Caring for your Pottery
paragraphs: You don’t have any items in your cart.
Only food-safe glazes are used on my pottery’s food surfaces.  I do not use any lead.
This is something I take great pride in.
I inspect each item to ensure the glaze was fired optimally.

This pottery is thrown using Canadian clay, and fired to 1204C / 2200 F. When cared for well, pottery of this quality can last decades and be passed down for generations. I hope you will enjoy these pieces for years and years, and therefore I recommend hand-washing, and not leaving your  pottery to sit/soak for long periods of time as all clay (even factory-made dishes) will absorb moisture over time.
Hand-wash your beautiful pottery and it will serve you many years of enjoyment.

I draw inspiration from connecting with the incredible and diverse natural beauty of Vancouver Island and the Salish Sea shores, which I am fortunate to call home.The name Artisanal Arsonist is derived from a back-country experience with friends, as a result of my campfire building skills. I take great joy in “firing” my art and hope that you love your pottery for many years to come.I am delighted to share the results of my deeply rooted creativity with you.
Only food-safe glazes are used on my pottery’s food surfaces.  I do not use any lead.
This is something I take great pride in.
I inspect each item to ensure the glaze was fired optimally.

This pottery is thrown using Canadian clay, and fired to 1204C / 2200F. When cared for well, pottery of this quality can last decades and be passed down for generations. I hope you will enjoy these pieces for years and years, and therefore I recommend hand-washing, and not leaving your  pottery to sit/soak for long periods of time as all clay (even factory-made dishes) will absorb moisture over time.
Hand-wash your beautiful pottery and it will serve you many years of enjoyment.

I respectfully acknowledge and cherish the privilege I have to live, work, play, and love, on the  territories of the Coast Salish Peoples, including the traditional territories of the Snaw-Naw-As, the Qualicum, and the K’ómoks First Nations.

pages.title: Shop | Artisanal Arsonist Pottery
paragraphs: You don’t have any items in your cart.

I am a small batch potter.  I may offer my pottery online one day, however, at this time my collections are small and I am cherishing the connection that I feel from the in-personinteractions of selling at markets.Please contact me directly if you have interest in a private sale.
